bio.biography:
Quick Facts

  • Specialty: Giant Slalom

  • Son of Mark and Mary Ellen Ford

  • He has an older brother, Tyson

  • His parents were both ski racers and coaches

  • Began skiing at age two

  • Enjoys rock climbing, drawing, mountain biking, photography and skateboarding 

Biographical Information 

Tommy Ford is a prominent American alpine skier specializing in giant slalom. Raised in Bend, Oregon, he was introduced to skiing at the age of two by his parents, both of whom were ski racers and coaches. This early exposure fostered a deep passion for the sport, leading him to competitive racing during his teenage years. 

Ford's talent became evident as he secured four gold medals at the 2006 U.S. Junior Championships. He officially joined the U.S. Ski Team in 2009, marking the beginning of his professional career. His world cup debut that same year saw him scoring points in his rookie season, and he earned a spot on the team for giant slalom at the Olympic Winter Games Vancouver 2010. A notable achievement in his career, Ford won his first world cup victory in December 2019, placing first in the giant slalom event. With this win, Ford became the first U.S. man to win a world cup since 2017.

Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 12th (giant slalom)
  • Years of participation: 2011, 2015, 2017, 2019
  • Top finish: 12th – 2019 (giant slalom)

USA Swimming

bio.fun_fact: Fascinated by World War II history, which is inspired by how his paternal grandparents met
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Speciality: Slopestyle, Big Air
  • Son of Ray and Anne Marie Forehand
  • Older sister, Savannah, who was a competitive mogul skier
  • Fascinated by World War II history, inspired by his paternal grandparents, Jack and Lorraine Forehand, who met in Europe during the war when they were in the U.S. Army
  • Loves surfing and mountain biking
Biographical Information

At the age of six, Mac Forehand followed his older sister into mogul skiing. The family would travel from Connecticut to Vermont to ski, before relocating to Vermont, so that both children could attend Stratton Mountain School, a ski academy founded in 1972 that has sent at least one athlete to the Winter Olympics since 1976. Forehand switched to slopestyle and park skiing at age 12 and quickly burst onto the scene as a teenager as one of the best in the world.

As a rookie on the U.S. ski team, Mac Forehand, at age 17, won the Crystal Globe in slopestyle. A few years later, he would make his Olympic debut at Beijing Games, finishing 11th in the big air and not qualifying for the slopestyle final. Since the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, Forehand has won four X Games medals including winning the big air event in 2023, his second Crystal globe in slopestyle for the 2023-24 season, and silver medal in slopestyle at the 2025 FIS Snowboard and Freeski World Championships.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, silver (Big Air - Men), 11th (Slopestyle - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 11th (Big Air - Men), 20th (Slopestyle - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – silver (Slopestyle - Men), 4th (Big Air - Men)
  • Years of participation: Big Air - Men 2021, 2025; Slopestyle - Men 2019, 2021, 2023, 2025
  • Medals: 1 (1 silver)
    • Silver – 2025 (Slopestyle - Men)

bio.education: Murrieta Valley High School (Murrieta, Calif.) '07 Oklahoma State University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Rod and Lynn Fowler
  • Gave up serious dirt-bike riding after an accident prior to his freshman year in high school resulted in several broken bones in his foot. His father Rod won the 1986 Baja 1000 while racing for a Yamaha team
  • Turned professional in 2009
  • Established the Rickie Fowler Foundation in 2011 to help aid both the Japanese and Native American communities. The Foundation focuses on expanding educational opportunities surrounding each separate heritage and offers opportunities for at-risk youth.
Career Highlights
  • 2010 PGA TOUR Rookie of the Year
  • 2015 Deutsche Bank Championship, 1st
  • 2015 THE PLAYERS Championship, 1st
  • 2012 Wells Fargo Championship, 1st
  • 2016, Abu Dhabi HSBC Championship, 1st (European Tour)
  • 2015 Aberdeen Asset Management Scottish Open, 1st (European Tour)
  • 2011 Kolon Korea Open, 1st (OneAsia Tour)
  • One Presidents Cup team (2015) and two Ryder Cup teams (2010, 2014) representing the United States
  • Two-time first-team NCAA All-American (2008, 2009)
  • Ben Hogan Award winner as the nation's top collegian (2008)
  • Phil Mickelson Award winner as the nation's top collegiate freshman (2008)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 37th (Individual - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 40th (Individual - Men)
  • Years of participation: Individual - Men 2022
  • Top finish: 40th – 2022 (Individual - Men)

Career Highlights
  • Pan American Games, Toronto, 2015 (Team - Women)
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Andrea and William Fowler
  • Has one brother, Jake
  • Hobbies include hiking, horseback riding, and arts and crafts.

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Prefers to be called “AD”
  • Grew up in a small town in Kansas with her mother Raqual Franch and two siblings, her brother Malik Veal and sister Patricia
  • Getting a home from Habitat for Humanity changed their lives
  • The first player from Kansas to make a U.S. Women's World Cup Team
  • Still describes herself as a small-town girl at heart
  • Listens to country before every game
  • Eats home-style southern breakfasts of biscuits and gravy in the mornings as often as she can
  • Married Emily Boscacci in 2019.
Career Highlights
  • 2019 SheBelieves Cup
  • Set the record for shut-outs at Oklahoma State with 36, the sixth most in NCAA history
  • First OSU player to make the U.S. Women’s National Team
  • The 2017 and 2018 NWSL Goalkeeper of the Year, the first player to ever win the award twice
